title: Support for machines without Internet access
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

In China, the connection to a international server like `docker.io` or `k8s.gcr.io` is unstable. So Chinese users prefer a mirror site for the docker hub registries, like `hub-mirror.c.163.com`, which server is located in China.

However, in podman, it requires stable Internet connection to `k8s.gcr.io`. There is no way for users to specify custom docker hub registries. So podman can not be used off the Internet.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman pod create --name=docker --share net -p 443:443 -p 80:80 -p 127.0.0.1:888:80
WARN[0042] failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get "https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/": dial tcp 64.233.189.82:443: connect: connection refused 
WARN[0085] failed, retrying in 2s ... (2/3). Error: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get "https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/": dial tcp 64.233.189.82:443: connect: connection refused 
WARN[0129] failed, retrying in 4s ... (3/3). Error: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get "https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/": dial tcp 64.233.189.82:443: connect: connection refused 
ERRO[0175] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory 
Error: error adding Infra Container: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get "https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/": dial tcp 74.125.203.82:443: connect: connection refused
```

**Describe the results you received:**
The podman command will not run successfully without Internet connection.

**Describe the results you expected:**
The podman command runs successfully without Internet connection.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  kernel: 5.10.0-8-amd64
  linkmode: dynamic

    name: crun

      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

A network where only a docker registry mirror is accessible.

comments.body:
You can change the infra image with `podman pod create --infra-image ...`.
You can also set this in the container.conf file:
```
$ grep infra /usr/share/containers/containers.conf
# Default command to run the infra container
# infra_command = "/pause"
# Infra (pause) container image name for pod infra containers.  When running a
# infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.4.1"
```
I believe @vrothberg has a card to ship the pause binary with Podman itself, so we can build the image locally.
Shouldn't we just ship a pause executable in /usr/libexec/podman/pause.  And then just setup a scratch container to use it, and we are done?

> Shouldn't we just ship a pause executable in /usr/libexec/podman/pause. And then just setup a scratch container to use it, and we are done?

We could do that but I still want to investigate whether we could embed this binary in Podman itself to avoid adding YAB (yet another binary) to the packages.

I am going to reopen since the workaround mentioned above (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11340#issuecomment-907229317) is a just a workaround.
As long as the embeded binary ends  up being tiny, when running.

We've run into this issue on a RHEL 7.9 machine with podman 1.6.4 and no internet connection. Here's what we do :

- build with Docker
- deploy to a CENTOS 8 machine with podman 3.0.2-dev for testing
- `podman save > server.tar abcdef123456`
- deploy server.tar to the production RHEL 7.9 machine with podman 1.6.4
- `podman load -i server.tar`
- `podman play kube server-pod.yaml`

which results in :

```sh
ERRO[0060] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory
Error: error adding Infra Container: unable to pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: unable to pull image: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/: dial tcp ip.add.re.ss:443: i/o timeout
```
we're going to test a podman save/load with `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1` to see if that works. I'll edit this post with what happens.

Is this a way to work round the issue?
What is the image tagged as once loaded?

> 
> 
> We've run into this issue on a RHEL 7.9 machine with podman 1.6.4 and no internet connection. Here's what we do :
> 
>     * build with Docker
> 
>     * deploy to a CENTOS 8 machine with podman 3.0.2-dev for testing
> 
>     * `podman save > server.tar abcdef123456`
> 
>     * deploy server.tar to the production RHEL 7.9 machine with podman 1.6.4
> 
>     * `podman load -i server.tar`
> 
>     * `podman play kube server-pod.yaml`
> 
> 
> which results in :
> 
> ```shell
> ERRO[0060] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory
> Error: error adding Infra Container: unable to pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: unable to pull image: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io: Get https://k8s.gcr.io/v2/: dial tcp ip.add.re.ss:443: i/o timeout
> ```
> 
> we're going to test a podman save/load with `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1` to see if that works. I'll edit this post with what happens.
> 
> Is this a way to work round the issue?

@DJDaveMark, Currently, the only workaround is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11340#issuecomment-907229317

i.e. do the following:
1. Mirror the image `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1` to your own docker repository server (that is accessible to your intranet network). In my example, I will use `ustc-edu-cn.mirror.aliyuncs.com` as the mirror server.
2. Copy `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf` to `/etc/containers/containers.conf` if the latter doesn't exist.
3. Edit the file `/etc/containers/containers.conf`:
```ini
[engine]
infra_command = "/pause"
infra_image = "ustc-edu-cn.mirror.aliyuncs.com/aiotceo/pause:3.1"
```

But apparently it is only a workaround. Frankly speaking, gross.
Could you try on a more modern version of Podman (On a newer OS), we know that RHEL7 has no access to newer Podman. This should be opened as a bugzilla, if the latest Podman works correctly.

> Edit the file /etc/containers/containers.conf:

you might edit the file to make it "local/pause" and that would work offline (after obtaining that image).

No. I mean, “by design”. Users can make some workaround, but a tool should not rely on these online resources. A stable resources that the tool always relies on should be delivered as a part of the tool itself.
the question is how to deliver a container from a file (like `/usr/libexec/podman/pause`) shipped by rpm?

> 
> 
> the question is how to deliver a container from a file (like `/usr/libexec/podman/pause`) shipped by rpm?

Yes. I think this is an acceptable solution.
I agree, I would like us to ship the pause program and then create a container off of it.

As a temporary workaround would it work if on a machine with an internet connection I do
* `podman pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1`
* `podman save > k8s-pause-3-1.tar abcdef123456`

deploy the tar file to the machine without internet access then:
* `podman load -i k8s-pause-3-1.tar`

then edit `/etc/containers/containers.conf` with:
```ini
[engine]
infra_command = "/pause"
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
```

> 
> 
> As a temporary workaround would it work if on a machine with an internet connection I do
> 
>     * `podman pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1`
> 
>     * `podman save > k8s-pause-3-1.tar abcdef123456`
> 
> 
> deploy the tar file to the machine without internet access then:
> 
>     * `podman load -i k8s-pause-3-1.tar`
> 
> 
> then edit `/etc/containers/containers.conf` with:
> 
> ```ini
> [engine]
> infra_command = "/pause"
> infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
> ```

Yes. It seems to be a temporary workaround for now. I think this issue is mainly focus on the design level, i.e. users should do nothing to let podman get rid of such an online dependency.

@vrothberg @rhatdan Afaik I think we are building `pause` locally now. Could this be closed.

Edit: I think it is not released yet. Nvm Please ignore this.
Good catch, @flouthoc. Yes, this issue has been resolved.

> Edit: I think it is not released yet. Nvm Please ignore this.

We can close issues once they're merged into the main branch.

> Shouldn't we just ship a pause executable in /usr/libexec/podman/pause. And then just setup a scratch container to use it, and we are done?

This is causing lot of pain for using podman in corporate env - can this be done quickly ? Pleeeeeease.
@kishorekkota, good news, it has been done and is merged into the main branch.  It will be shipped with the next release of Podman which will be early next year.
We might need to add this to podman 3.5 rather then waiting for podman 4, if we want to get it into RHEL sooner.
Pulling in @mheon.  @rhatdan, did you mean 3.4?

There is no chance that this makes it into 3.5.

There is a chance we do a 3.5, but it will exclusively be OS X fixes and features, and it will continue to be based off the 3.4 branch with backports. We still need to work out the timing on 4.0 and a potential 3.5.

Do i need to do this on local MacOS or does this need to be run in PODMAN MACHINE itself. I see PODMAN MACHINE running as a linux container which delegates / executes all the command issued on the local machine ( MacOS in my case ). I am a bit confused on architecture. Is there documentation with visuals to help understand the architecture ?

@kishorekkota The config change should be done in the VM, for the podman commands it should not matter.

This blog might help you to understand the architecture: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-mac-machine-architecture
